diagrams.title: Row vs Column Storage Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows how data is physically stored on disk - row-based stores complete rows together while column-based stores all values of each column contiguously
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why column-based databases excel at analytical queries requiring aggregation on single columns

diagrams.title: Cassandra Cluster Architecture
diagrams.description: Peer-to-peer ring architecture showing data distribution across multiple nodes without a master node
diagrams.purpose: Understanding distributed, masterless architecture of Cassandra and how requests are routed

diagrams.title: Cassandra Write Path Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step flow of data from write request to persistent storage
diagrams.purpose: Understanding durability mechanism and how Cassandra achieves high write throughput

diagrams.title: Cassandra Read Path Flow
diagrams.description: How reads are serviced from memory and disk using multiple optimization structures
diagrams.purpose: Understanding read optimization and why Bloom filters improve performance

diagrams.title: CAP Theorem Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the three properties of CAP theorem and the tradeoffs between them in distributed systems
elements: Triangle with Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why Cassandra prioritizes availability and how tunable consistency works
